intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.details: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as living specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only remaining biological entity, though in a state of severe decline.

inventory_reconciliation.p3_black_pot: Present (Pothos cutting with white rabbit anchor)

p3_pothos.observation: The single leaf is chlorotic and shows signs of severe dehydration and turgor loss. The petiole is thin and lacks structural integrity.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in the black pot (P3/P4 area) acting as a soil amendment or calcium supplement. No fungal growth detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-3: Initial state shows a single, struggling Pothos leaf. Soil is dark.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: Progressive loss of light and clarity in the image sequence makes detailed observation difficult, but the Pothos leaf appears to have lost its original posture, leaning further into the substrate.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of terminal decline. The lack of light (as evidenced by the extreme underexposure in recent frames) and potential substrate nutrient depletion have rendered the environment hostile to the registered species.
anomalies: Extreme underexposure in recent images (lighting failure)
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is failing. The Pothos (P3) is the sole survivor of the initial registry, and it is currently exhibiting signs of severe physiological stress. The other pots (P1, P2, P4) appear to be devoid of their intended occupants, suggesting a total loss of the original collection. The environment is dark, likely contributing to the rapid decline of the remaining specimen.
